data.title: Musk’s X faces Irish data protection probe over sexualized images generated by Grok AI
data.description:
• Ireland’s data protection regulator has opened an inquiry into X amid concerns about sexualized images generated and shared through the Grok AI chatbot.
  • The probe will determine whether billionaire Elon Musk’s social media platform has complied with its obligations regarding the processing of European Union users’ personal data, according to a statement by the Dublin-based Data Protection Commission on Tuesday.
  • The investigation has been initiated under the Data Protection Act.
  • "As the Lead Supervisory Authority for XIUC across the EU/EEA, the DPC has commenced a large-scale inquiry which will examine XIUC’s compliance with some of their fundamental obligations under the GDPR in relation to the matters at hand," Deputy Commissioner Graham Doyle said.
  • Last month, the European Commission launched an investigation into xAI, which developed the Grok chatbot, for its ability to create sexualized deepfakes of women and minors nonconsensually, in violation of the bloc's Digital Services Act. The UK's Ofcom also launched a similar probe into Grok.

data.title: BHP signs $4.3B silver streaming deal with Wheaton Precious Metals
data.description:
BHP (BHP) surges 6% to an all-time high in Australian trading early Tuesday after saying it signed a long-term silver streaming agreement with Wheaton Precious Metals (WPM) for an upfront payment of US$4.3B at completion, plus ongoing payments for silver ounces delivered equal to 20% of the spot price of silver.

In exchange, BHP (BHP) said it will deliver silver to Wheaton (WPM) from its share of production at Peru's Antamina mine, where it has a 33.75% investment in mine operator Compañía Minera Antamina.

The US$4.3B payment is part of a targeted US$10B that BHP (BHP) aims to raise from existing assets, which also includes a US$2B Australian power infrastructure deal signed in December, CEO Mike Henry said.

Upon closing, Wheaton (WPM) said it will receive a combined 67.5% of all the silver produced from Antamina, up from the 33.75% currently delivered under the existing Glencore (GLCNF) (GLNCY) silver stream.

"Quality silver production is becoming increasingly difficult to source while demand continues to rise for both critical industrial uses and for silver's safe haven qualities in today's economic environment," Wheaton (WPM) CEO Randy Smallwood said.

BHP (BHP) also reported stronger-than-expected underlying profit for the six months through December, driven by copper, which for the first time surpassed iron ore in the miner's earnings.

H1 underlying attributable profit rose 22% Y/Y to US$6.2B, beating the Visible Alpha consensus of US$6.03B, and the company declared an interim dividend of US$0.73/share, ahead of market expectations of US$0.63/share, representing a 60% payout ratio and up from US$0.50/share in the year-earlier period.

BHP's (BHP) H1 output of copper, including byproducts such as gold, contributed US$7.95B to its operating earnings, above iron ore's US$7.5B and comprising 51% of the group's total underlying operating earnings of US$15.46B, driven largely by a 32% jump in realized prices for copper.

The dividend hike reflects "strong operating performance, disciplined capital allocation, and confidence in our outlook," Henry said.

data.title: Sonoco Products Non-GAAP EPS of $1.05 beats by $0.05, revenue of $1.77B beats by $10M
data.description:
• Sonoco Products press release (SON): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.05 beats by $0.05.
  • Revenue of $1.77B (+30.1% Y/Y) beats by $10M.
  • Grew fourth quarter net sales to $1.8 billion, up 29.7% from the prior-year quarter, primarily from acquisition activity
  • Targeting full-year adjusted diluted earnings per share of $5.80 to $6.20. Full-year adjusted EBITDA is expected to be $1.25 billion to $1.35 billion. Cash flows from operating activities are expected to be $700 million to $800 million.
  • The Company will continue to simplify its operating and reporting structure in 2026 and will only report its results in two segments, Consumer Packaging and Industrial Paper Packaging. The Company’s industrial plastics packaging business, which was the only remaining business in All Other, will be included in the Industrial Paper Packaging segment. The Company believes this reporting structure appropriately represents the management of its business portfolio going forward.
  •  
  • Shares +2.3% AH.

data.title: Armada Hoffler Properties FFO of $0.29 beats by $0.03, revenue of $75.6M beats by $6.98M

data.title: SA Asks: Which hardware stocks are best positioned for AI?
data.description:
Which hardware stocks are best positioned for the AI revolution?

Seeking Alpha analysts Michael Del Monte, Kennedy Njagi, and Elizabeth Pramila offer their picks.

Michael Del Monte: My top names for hardware largely reside in the “gray space” of data center infrastructure, including IES (IESC) and Vertiv (VRT). IESC largely focuses on the installation and custom design of electrical and technology systems found in data centers. Vertiv is in a unique position of integrating the white and gray spaces within the data center, providing integrated rack-scale solutions as well as power orchestration software. 

Other hardware considerations include Texas Instruments (TXN) and Analog Devices (ADI), given the growing importance of power management solutions.

Kennedy Njagi: The way I look at AI is that the real monetization bottleneck sits on one layer below the apps, in memory and bandwidth. And that’s why Micron Technology (MU) has been on my radar for quite a while now. This is because every serious training or inference workload runs into a memory wall. Yet when supply tightens, the industry doesn’t need a marketing department to show up in the numbers. 

Marvell Technology (MRVL) and Credo Technology (CRDO) are another pair I keep circling. The entire AI buildout turns into a networking and interconnect problem once you scale beyond a few racks, and high-speed connectivity is where performance and cost collide fast.

Astera Labs (ALAB) also fits the same “plumbing matters” theme, with PCIe and CXL connectivity that helps CPUs, accelerators, and memory talk to each other without the system spending half its life waiting. 

None of these are flashy stories, but they’re the toll booths on the AI highway. And the checks tend to clear there first when CapEx ramps.

Of course, there’s still cyclicality and execution risk, so I’m not pretending this is a straight line. In chess terms, MU, MRVL, CRDO, and ALAB are the pieces controlling the center squares of the AI infrastructure board, and if you own the center, you usually get to decide how the game is played! 

Elizabeth Pramila: It is always prudent to view investment opportunities from a risk POV, and when seen through that lens, Nvidia (NVDA)—everyone's obvious choice for AI hardware—is actually the riskiest of all. I have discussed customer concentration, emerging competition, and ROI risks for key clients in past articles, which I invite you to read. 

At the other end of the hardware spectrum is Apple (AAPL), which has clearly pivoted to a third-party strategy that allows it to deploy the latest AI capabilities on its huge install base. This is a low-risk opportunity that still has plenty of steam for a long growth runway.

Apple Intelligence may no longer be an in-house effort, but I foresee it making significant strides from an AI adoption perspective, the first signs being the partnership with Alphabet (GOOG) (GOOGL) for Gemini deployment on Apple hardware. The biggest risk-mitigation factor here is that there is no pressure on AAPL to invest in AI CapEx, a bold deviation from what other mega-caps are doing. 

In between these two extremes are several other AI hardware opportunities, such as Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), Amazon (AMZN), Alphabet (GOOG) (GOOGL), Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM), and ASML (ASML), all of which I have written about on Seeking Alpha. 

If I were forced to give you just one takeaway for investing in AI-focused hardware stocks, it would be this: AI development is and will be an iterative process, so the fastest growers now might end up dropping out of the race once their edge has worn off.

The real long-term investment opportunity is with companies like Apple, Alphabet, Amazon, and others that not only make their own AI hardware but also possess vast delivery ecosystems through their devices and cloud-based AI infrastructure. It is consumer spending on AI that will see the strongest growth in the long run, so my advice would be to focus on stocks that can either exhibit signs of sustainable ROIC expansion or have a ready install base or cloud-and-edge infrastructure through which AI capabilities are served. 

This is regardless of whether the opportunity is upstream, as with ASML and TSM; midstream; or downstream, such as AAPL, AMZN, and GOOG. There are too many to list, but hopefully the criteria I have laid out will create a framework for your own process of elimination.

  • Top Semiconductor Stocks
  • Top Electrical Components & Equipment Stocks
  • Top Construction & Engineering Stocks
  • Top Trending AI Stocks

data.title: Quant ratings on Ray Dalio’s Bridgewater top holdings: NVDA, LRCX, CRM, GOOGL
data.description:
Bridgewater Associates, led by Ray Dalio, posted a 12.41% gain over the past year, slightly outperforming the S&P 500’s 12% return, according to data from stockcircle.com.

The fund primarily focused on information technology stocks, with notable holdings in finance, consumer discretionary, communications, industrials, and health care sectors, the latest 13F filing showed.

Comparing Bridgewater Associates’ holdings between Q4 2024 and Q4 2025 shows notable shifts in both position size and allocation. The number of shares held in NVIDIA (NVDA) increased from 3.50M shares to 3.87M, up 11%. Lam Research (LRCX) saw a 20% rise year-over-year, growing from 2.53M to 3.04M shares. Salesforce (CRM) experienced the most dramatic growth, soaring 285% year-over-year from 502.4K to 1.93M shares.

In contrast, Alphabet (GOOGL) was trimmed significantly, dropping 56% year-over-year from 3.62M to 1.59M shares. Microsoft (MSFT) saw a moderate increase, rising from 667K to 984.1K shares, reflecting a steady upward adjustment. Overall, the fund sharply increased exposure to key tech names like NVDA, LRCX, and CRM while reducing stakes in GOOGL.

The fund’s top holdings as a percentage of the portfolio are State Street SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (SPY) at 11.08%, Nvidia (NVDA) at 2.63%, Amazon (AMZN) at 1.64%, iShares Core S&P 500 ETF (IVV) at 10.45%, and Google (GOOGL) (GOOG) at 1.82%.

Seeking Alpha looked at the holdings and ranked its top 15 constituents according to their Quant Ratings.

  • State Street SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (SPY) – Quant rating: 3.57 – Buy
  • iShares Core S&P 500 ETF (IVV) — Quant rating: 3.54 – Buy
  • NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) – Quant rating: 3.49 – Hold
  • Lam Research Corporation (LRCX) – Quant rating: 3.49 – Hold
  • Salesforce, Inc. (CRM) – Quant rating: 3.38 – Hold
  • Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) – Quant rating: 3.48 – Hold
  • Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) – Quant rating: 3.48 – Hold
  • Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) – Quant rating: 3.40 – Hold
  • Adobe Inc. (ADBE) – Quant rating: 2.90 – Hold
  • GE Vernova Inc. (GEV) – Quant rating: 3.46 – Hold
  • Booking Holdings Inc. (BKNG) – Quant rating: 3.05 – Hold
  • Broadcom Inc. (AVGO) – Quant rating: 3.49 – Hold
  • Oracle Corporation (ORCL) – Quant rating: 3.44 – Hold
  • Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. (AMD) – Quant rating: 4.68 – Strong Buy
  • Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) – Quant rating: 3.45 – Hold

data.title: Most and least shorted healthcare stocks with up to $2B market cap
data.description:
Short interest in small-cap healthcare surged toward the end of January, centering heavily on the biotechnology sector. Data as of January 30 shows that KalVista (KALV) and Intellia (NTLA) have become the most shorted names in the $2 billion-and-under market cap tier, carrying short interest ratios of 39.23% and 36.74%, respectively.

Conversely, the "least-shorted" stocks, such as Syra Health (SYRA) and Ernexa Therapeutics (ERNA) currently maintain negligible short interest of just 0.51%, signaling stability among small-cap investors.

Healthcare stocks are outperforming the broader market so far this year. The Health Care Select Sector SPDR® Fund ETF (NYSEARCA:XLV), which tracks the health care sector and holds a weightage of 12.12% in the S&P 500 index, has gained 1.85% in 2026. This stands in contrast to the S&P 500's overall 0.14% decline, signaling investor preference for healthcare over growth sectors.

Here are the five most shorted healthcare stocks with market capitalizations of up to $2 billion (as a % of shares outstanding) as of January 30:

KalVista Pharmaceuticals (KALV) Biotechnology sector, short interest 39.23%               

Intellia Therapeutics (NTLA) Biotechnology sector, short interest 36.74%                          

Novavax (NVAX) Biotechnology sector, short interest 32.79%                      

Recursion Pharmaceuticals (RXRX) Biotechnology sector, short interest 31.77%                          

Tango Therapeutics (TNGX) Biotechnology sector, short interest 31.75%

Syra Health (SYRA) Health Care Services, short interest 0.51%       

Ernexa Therapeutics (ERNA) Biotechnology sector, short interest 0.51%                    

Aprea Therapeutics (APRE) Biotechnology sector, short interest 0.51%                          

China Pharma Holdings (CPHI) Pharmaceuticals sector, short interest 0.52%                             

Modular Medical (MODD) Health Care Equipment sector, short interest 0.52%

Now read: Most and least shorted mid-to mega-cap healthcare stocks in January

Related ETFs: Health Care Select Sector SPDR Fund (NYSEARCA:XLV), SPDR S&P Pharmaceuticals ETF (NYSEARCA:XPH), VanEck Pharmaceutical ETF (NASDAQ:PPH), iShares Nasdaq Biotechnology ETF (NASDAQ:IBB), iShares U.S. Pharmaceuticals ETF (NYSEARCA:IHE), and Invesco Nasdaq Biotechnology ETF (NASDAQ:IBBQ).

data.title: Crypto company Nexo returns to US in partnership with Bakkt
data.description:
Crypto lending platform Nexo on Monday announced a relaunch in the U.S. in partnership with digital asset solutions provider Bakkt (BKKT).

In January 2023, Nexo had agreed to pay the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission a penalty of $45M. The fine was charged over a failure to register the offer and sale of its retail crypto asset lending product, the Earn Interest Product.

EIP allowed U.S. investors to tender their crypto assets to the company in exchange for interest. The SEC order had found that the EIP is a security and its offer and sale did not qualify for an exemption from registration.

The company has returned three years after leaving the U.S. following clashes with regulators, a Monday report by Reuters noted.

Nexo has discontinued the EIP product for U.S. investors, a spokesperson reportedly told the newswire.

Its U.S. offering includes flexible and fixed-term yield programs, an integrated exchange, crypto-backed credit lines, and a loyalty program.

Bakkt is set to provide the digital asset trading infrastructure in the partnership.
